heading: Decrease Capacity or Throughput
body:
- AWS (Amazon Web Services) doesn't allow you to reduce storage *capacity* in place.

  Atlas *can* reduce storage capacity by first provisioning new
  volumes and then syncing data from the old to the new volumes with an
  in-place rsync process. This works around the AWS (Amazon Web Services) limitation, but
  there is downtime on each node while this process is in progress.

- AWS (Amazon Web Services) *does* allow you to reduce IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) without migrating data.

  For disk size decreases, Atlas always provisions new volumes and
  syncs data from the old to the new volumes. This process requires downtime
  on each node during the rsync operation, regardless of the volume
  modification cooldown period.
